DISPOSAL OF THIS SCRIPT COPY DOES NOT ALTER ANY OF THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH ABOVE. We hear the faint sound of CAR TIRES running over the CONCRETE SEAMS of a highway. Eventually,...
INT. STATION WAGON - DAY

CLOSE ON DUNCAN, staring off, lost in thought. Some SUITCASES and COOLERS flank him. It's a little cramped. PULL BACK to reveal he's sitting in that ill-conceived back bench seat that faces out the rear of a vintage 1971 Buick Estate station wagon. MAN'S VOICE (O.S.)
CLOSE ON REARVIEW MIRROR.
TRENT RAMSEY (MAN'S VOICE) glances
Duncan, are you sleeping? Clearly, Duncan wishes he was.
No.
DUNCAN'S POV of a SUV of TEENAGERS passing by. flip him off.
Let me ask you something. On a scale from one to ten, what do you think you are? Beat.
Duncan? I'm asking you how you see yourself. On a scale from one to
I don't know. What?
You need to speak up, buddy.
They gawk,
(louder) I don't know.
DUNCAN'S POV of a BILLBOARD on the opposite side of the highway. It reads, "Turn around! You missed it! Water Wizz Waterpark! Two miles back!"
What don't you know? How you see yourself? You don't have an opinion?
Duncan doesn't respond.
I'm just curious. Pick any number on a scale...
What?
Trent takes this in.
I think you're a three. Damn.
Duncan definitely doesn't know how to respond.
Do you know why I think you're a three? Do you know what would make me say that? (beat) Duncan...? No.
You don't know why? idea? No.
You have no
You've got to speak up.
Duncan turns all the way around, making eye contact with Trent in the rearview mirror.
(louder)
Well, from what I've observed, I can't say I see you putting yourself out there, buddy.
From this vantage point, Duncan surveys the landscape.
listening to her iPod, is STEPH...
Meeting kids your own age. And, from what your mom tells me, you seem content just to hang around her apartment. ... And, riding shotgun, his mom, PAM.
She's asleep as well.
Is that a fair assessment? That you're happy to just... Not do anything? Because, see, that's a "three" to me.
Duncan glances back at Trent in the mirror.

But, the good news is I can tell you that there's going to be plenty of kids, and plenty of opportunities at the beach for you to take advantage of this summer. And, it's a big summer for all of us, really. See if we can make this work... You and your Mom, me and Steph... All making it work together. As a family. (beat) So, what do you say? Let's try to improve that score. Aim higher than a three?
Duncan sits back down, facing out the back.
That sound good? (beat) You up for that, buddy?
Grabs his iPod. He puts his earphones in, as we...
EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY The station wagon, in all its glory. Mint condition.

Strapped to the top, more BAGS. As for the rest of the cars on the highway, very much 2012. The station wagon pulls off the EXIT RAMP. Another BILLBOARD reads, "This is it! This is the exit! Water Wizz Waterpark!"
EXT. STREET - DAY

The station wagon drives along the main street. A quaint beach town. Dated. TOURISTS/LOCALS, flood the sidewalks. The station wagon arrives at a... STOP SIGN. A CONVERTIBLE pulls up behind. The DRIVER is wearing classic Vaurnet sunglasses. Although retro, they work for him. The Driver looks right at Duncan, enjoying the awkwardness. Duncan doesn't know where else to look. The Driver nods, smiles. The station wagon continues straight, as the convertible turns off to the left, screeching away.
EXT. BEACH HOUSE - DAY
The station wagon pulls into the DRIVEWAY. Beach front. Prime real estate. The house, a very masculine vibe. END MAIN TITLES
EXT. BEACH HOUSE - FRONT YARD - DAY - MOMENTS LATER

Trent unpacks. Duncan takes in his "home" for the summer. Pam steps up behind him, puts her hand on his shoulder. This is our first good view of Pam, wearing a stylish HEAD SCARF. In fact, her whole look is unique. Her own. Duncan and Pam notice the "name" of the house over the DOORWAY. "Riptide." Suddenly, BETTY, pops out, a Greyhound cocktail in hand. house's name: "Booze Cruizin'." Yoohoo!
(under his breath) Are you kidding? We just got here.
Betty descends the stairs.

Thank God! One more night of drinking alone and I was going to kill myself! (then, re: cocktail) Yes, I'm off the wagon again. Accept it and move on. (then, re: station wagon) Nice ride, Trent. Does the eight track still work?
(smiling) What? This is exactly the car my dad used to drive us in. Huh.
Old and shitty.
You know how much I paid for this? It's in cherry condition.
A car salesman with bad taste. Shocker.
Betty turns to Pam, invading her space. Hi.
I'm Betty. Hi,... Pam...
Trent's probably already talked shit about me. It's okay if he has. Has he? What did he say?
No, um... We were just...
(off scarf) I'm loving this. I couldn't pull it off. My hair seems to hate me.
Oh, thank you...
(to Trent) I like her.
(giving up) ... Okay.
Pam goes to pull a suitcase out of the car.
What's not to like?
Pam catches Trent, ogling. Embarrassed, she looks at Betty.
Oh, honey. Get it where you can. I'd be on my back after a line like that.
Just then, we hear a SCREEN DOOR CLOSE. Betty looks up to find SUSANNA THOMPSON, her daughter, standing on their deck. She's quite cute. A very "mature beyond her years" look.
Susanna, Trent's here. And this is
his girlfriend, Pam. Say, "Hi." Duncan, curious, peeks over the top of the car.
Beat.
Susanna stays put. Betty waits for more.

(sarcastic) Yeah, but don't come down. (to Pam/Trent, sotto) Just stand there, brooding. (then, noticing Duncan) Oh. You've brought me a man. How thoughtful. Who is this in all his "awkward stage" glory?
Duncan embarrassed, peers over the top of the station wagon, seeing if Susanna heard that. DUNCAN'S POV of Susanna going back inside. Pam puts her arm around Duncan, pulls him in.
This is my son, Duncan.
She brushes the hair out of his eyes.

Oh my God. I was going to name my youngest "Duncan," but my exhusband thought it was nerdy. So, we went with "Peter." Which, personally, I think is worse. But, it fits my son, so... (then, to Duncan) Don't worry, you wear the name well. Trent, where's Steph?
Well, we've been here all of two minutes, so probably changing for the beach.
(to Pam) She's just like me. Screw cancer. I want to be an even golden brown. Everywhere.
Trent, Pam, and Duncan start lugging stuff inside. plenty to grab, but Betty doesn't notice.

Well, everybody's back this year. The Gales. The Hutchisons. The Campbells, sans Ben because of the trial. (then, to Pam) Insider trading. (then) The Smythes. Oh, and the Keegans, who I'm not talking to, but you can if you want, but just know that most of us aren't.
Pam passes the mailbox, glances at it. CLOSE ON mailbox. The side reads, "The Ramseys."
So, do you think you will?
Pam?
Do I think I will, what?
Talk to the Keegans.
Oh, I don't know. know them.
I don't even
Well, do if you want to, but know that I'm mad at them. I don't even want to get into why. They called me a C-U-N-T. (then, excited) You're going to love it here!
INT. BEACH HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY - MOMENTS LATER

Duncan sits on a bench. Betty plants herself on a stool and helps herself to a bag of POTATO CHIPS. Trent makes a Mount Gay and tonic. Pam, unfamiliar with the kitchen, attempts to put away groceries. It's a "trial by fire" project.
So, how was your winter?
Pam starts to respond, but...
Because our year was a challenge. My niece was raped in October...
Oh my goodness...

I know. Not even food courts are safe. Oh, and well, Bob, that's my ex-husband, finally came out of the closet. Not a shock. Let's just say that, in bed, his favorite view was the back of my head. Now, my oldest, Charlie, is still into the drugs and psychedelic music. The drugs I get. But, come on, Gary Garcia's dead! Let it go, already!
Betty laughs. Pam glances at Trent, mouthing the word "Gary?" Trent waves it off.
(to Duncan) Don't do drugs, Declan...
... Duncan...
(not hearing) ... They're just terrible. Oh, and Susanna. Well, you saw. She's a walking mood. Going through an "I hate my mother phase." Taking her father's side, as usual.
Steph enters, wearing the tiniest of tiny bikinis.
Oh, Steph, sweetie, don't you look great.
That's exactly the kind of suit that got me pregnant the first time.
That's what I'm hoping for. Hey,...
(rolling her eyes) I'm kidding!
Steph grabs a beer from the six-pack on the counter. Thrown, Pam glances at Trent, who seems oblivious. Pam takes her look to Betty, who commiserates.

I know. I can't drink beer anymore, either. (then, grabbing her sides) Look at these muffin tops. Oh, and my youngest, Peter... He had surgery to fix his lazy eye. Now, it's even worse. I try to get him to wear the eye patch, so people don't feel uncomfortable. But, what are you going to do?
Steph shoves a couple more beers into her beach bag.
I'm outta here.
Oh, hey, take Duncan with you.
Trent makes a point to look at Duncan, encouraging.
Fuck that noise. I told you I'm not baby-sitting him all summer.
You're taking Duncan with you, young lady.
Duncan looks at his Mom. "Do I not get a say?"
(off Duncan) Go to the beach, sweetie. (MORE)
You'll have fun. We're just going to be unpacking here.
(suggestive, playful) Well,...
Trent caresses Pam's ass, pulls her in and kisses her. placates, but is definitely blushing.

That's our cue, kids. (then) Oh. Now, I am still having our annual Fourth of July Clambake even though Bob's not here this year. He's probably sucking off our gardener right now. Okay well, let's have a fun summer!
Betty exits, taking the potato chips with her. Duncan glances at his mom, hoping to be saved. But, she's lost in Trent.
... You coming or what? Steph leaves.
Duncan shuffles out the door.
EXT. BEACH - WOODEN PATH - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
Steph strides along. lags behind. Duncan, still in jeans and a T-shirt,
Keep your distance. And, just don't die. I don't need that shit over my head.
EXT. BEACH - DAY - A LITTLE LATER

Steph sits in the sand with her friends, KATY and LAURA. Susanna is there as well, reading. Although the same age as Steph, Susanna has a maturity level that is leaps and bounds beyond the rest of the girls. Steph's boyfriend (or "summer friend"), CHAD, is playing football with some GUYS nearby. Seated off to the side, Duncan.
Oh hey, just so you guys know, my house is out this year for partying.
What?! Are you kidding?/I'm hating you right now! I'm serious.
My grandmother came up with us. So, my mom's like, "This could be her last summer alive." And I'm, like, "Yeah, well, I'm currently living, so..."
What a selfish bitch. I know.
This is, like, my summer.
Well, we can absolutely party at my place. My dad only gets to see me, twice a year, so he totally just wants me to be happy.
That's exactly the type of parent I'm going to be.
It just makes sense./I'm gonna do drugs with my kids.
Oh my God. I forgot. up with Jessica.
Are you serious?/Good. I don't get her face.
Susanna, you have to get on that this summer.
Steph hops up.
(calling off) Chad!
ANGLE ON Chad, preoccupied with his friends.
I want to go in the water! Then, go!
Steph just takes the moment in stride, brushes off. notices Duncan staring at them. She
What are you staring at, perv?
Steph walks off with Katy and Laura...
Where is that kid from, anyway?
Albany. He and his mom. They live in, like, a one bedroom apartment.
Eww!/Tragic.
(noticing) Uh, Susanna. We're all swimming!
Duncan watches Susanna reluctantly jump up, join them.
EXT. BEACH - WOODEN PATH - DAY - A LITTLE LATER
Duncan heads back toward the houses. From the opposite direction, Betty and her son, PETER. Clearly, Betty has transferred her cocktail to a Solo cup.
Duncan, we were just coming to find you. This is Peter.
Hey, Duncan. Hey.
Betty wasn't kidding. Peter's eye goes in every direction. Duncan doesn't know where to look.
Oh, just stare at the bridge of his nose. That's what I do. Mom.
Are you shitting me?

Well, if you wore the patch, I wouldn't have to keep telling people that, would I?! Duncan, I was thinking that you and Peter should hang out this summer. Lord knows he brought enough of his Star Wars dolls. You could have fun with those.

They're action figures! And they're classics! (then, to Duncan) I've got a rare wookie and the C3PO with detachable arms. They lose value if I take them out of the boxes, but we can still have awesome battles with them.
He needs human contact. He's having too many conversations with those dolls.
No, I'm not!
I've heard you, Peter! You called that "Baby Foot," or whatever, your best friend.
(not worth the effort) Boba Fett...
(ignoring, to Duncan) So, should we set up a time for you two to get together tomorrow?
Uh, I should check with my mom.
Duncan starts off.
(calling after) Okay, well, don't stand us up because we know where you live! (then, to Peter) See, I told you. Your eye makes people uncomfortable.
You're the worst parent.
INT./EXT. BEACH HOUSE (FRONT) - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
Duncan enters. From upstairs, PLAYFUL LAUGHING. Pam and Trent are clearly fooling around. With that, Duncan turns right around, and heads back out.
EXT. STATION WAGON - DAY - MOMENTS LATER

Duncan is lying on top of the roof, eyes closed, listening to his iPod. Unfortunately, he's listening to REO Speedwagon's "Can't Fight This Feeling." AND, he's singing out loud. He opens his eyes to see... Susanna standing beside the car, watching. Clearly, she's back from the beach. Duncan fumbles with the iPod, drops it. The earphones stay in, the console falls to the ground. Embarrassed, he looks over, only to see that... ... She's heading into her house.
INT. BEACH HOUSE - LIVING ROOM/DINING ROOM - DAY - LATE AFTERNOON

Duncan comes down the stairs. Pam is setting the dining room table for dinner. Trent, making drinks at the BAR. With them, KIP and JOAN, Trent's friends. "KYRIE," by MR. MISTER blares on the stereo. (We hope.) They're all trying to sing along, but clearly don't know the words. Joan is dancing, occasionally tugging on Kip and Trent to join her. But, to no avail. The song gets to the CHORUS. This part, Trent, Kip, and even Pam know well. However,...
(singing the chorus) "Carry a laser down the road that I must travel. Carry a laser through the darkness of the night..."
(laughing, shouting over the music) Whoa, whoa. Are you singing, "Carry a laser?"
That's what it is.
Trent, Pam, and Kip laugh.
Why would anyone in their right mind write a song called, "Carry A Laser?"
(laughing, shrugging) Because they love outer space.
She goes back to singing and dancing.
(noticing Duncan) Uh-oh. You just walked in at the wrong time, stranger.
Before Duncan can even protest, Joan is coaxing him onto her "dance floor."
This is Duncan. My son. These are
Trent's friends, Kip and Joan.
I'm the one not grinding up on you.
(laughing) I'm not grinding up on him. (to Duncan) Come on, I can't get anyone to dance with me.
Duncan looks to Pam for rescuing.
(smirking) Oh. I'll make it worse.

Pam joins in. Duncan halfheartedly gives in. A lot of awkward movement. At this point, everyone embraces the lyric "Carry a laser." Full on singing. Save for Trent, who puts on a good face, but is clearly over the moment. Steph descends the stairs.
I'm out of here. Oh, wait.
I made dinner. Oh, shoot.
I have plans.
Steph, check out his moves.
Steph watches for a short beat...
Enjoy therapy.
She grabs beers off the bar and goes.
Alright, we did that...
Trent turns down the music. Duncan, self-conscious, stops dancing. Pam grabs a PLATTER of HOR D'OURVES and BOX OF CRACKERS, places them on the COFFEE TABLE.
Hey, buddy, any chance you were on top of my car? ... No.
Without even being prompted, Duncan helps Pam, opening the CRACKERS.
Cause there was a dent in the roof. It popped back up. So, it's okay. I'm just wondering if you knew anything about that?
(innocent) Well, we did have bags up there, honey. Not the one he wanted to hear from, but...
That's true. We did. Fair enough.
Trent smiles for Pam's benefit.
(pointed, to Duncan) So, that's probably it.
He winks at Duncan.
Can I help you with this, too, Pam?
No. Thank you. (then, smiling at Duncan) We've kind of got a system. Oh.
Trent puts his arm around, Pam.
We've got a good team, here.
He kisses her cheek.
He seems proud. Duncan steps away,
INT. BEACH HOUSE - DINING ROOM - THAT NIGHT
Post dinner. Joan, holding court. food around on his plate. Duncan pushes remnants of
No, I can tell you exactly where I was. Passed out in the dunes. The guys laugh. gracious face.
Pam is out of the loop, but puts on a
That's right!
Because, you left me there!
No, now wait! Kip wanted to go out on his boat. He said you'd be fine by the fire.
You never came back!
(scoffing) The next day I did.
I poked you with a stick to wake you. It was real romantic. The guys laugh.
You know, I once went to this horrible luau-themed party...
(to Trent) Now, was it last summer...?
Oh, I'm sorry...
(faux sincere) No. Go. Please.
No, please. You were talking...
Tell your story, babe. Yeah.
Enough of us, reminiscing...
It's dumb...
Well, now we have to hear it.
(quickly) One time I went to this luau and passed out in the dunes, too. That's basically it.
Dead air. A flop of a story. But, everyone feigns enjoyment to ease the awkwardness for Pam.
(passively) ... Oh, how funny.
It was just... A similar thing to...
Trent pulls in Pam close. He's very attracted to this side of her. Kip starts piling more food onto his plate.
Okay, I just have to point out that I'm loading up for the third time. Pam, absolutely incredible.
No, thank you. (re: Trent) Sorry this asshole sprung us on you, last minute.
It's fine. I'm a caterer. cooking for people. You cater?
That makes sense.
Joan takes a sip of her wine, smiles.
One of her many talents.
Trent kisses her. Pam smiles, graciously. being put on a pedestal. It shows. She's not used to
She's also got some ideas for fixing up the place...
You mean,... (playing it up) ... the "Riptide?" Trent laughs, deflects.
He takes a lot of shit for this.
(clarifying) I mentioned rearranging a few things.
Hey. Put your stamp on it. not set in my ways.
(scoffing) Tell that to Christine.
Kip lightly nudges Joan. A little awkward silence, then...
(moving on) Okay. I'm sorry, I have to put my son out of his misery. (MORE) PAM (CONT'D) (to Duncan) You're free to go.
With that, Duncan jumps up from the table. Finally, relief.
But, take your plate, buddy. We clean up after ourselves in this house.
Duncan complies, as Trent pours Pam some more wine. watches her son head off to the KITCHEN, as...
Okay. My story now. Trent, was it last summer that you and I got stuck out on our boat?
(pointing to Kip) Yes, cause this bastard buys gas, three dollars at a time!
They all laugh, and continue to reminisce.
INT. BEACH HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER THAT NIGHT

Duncan looks around aimlessly for anything to pass the time. - Surveys the BOOKCASES. L'Amour-type novels. - Checks out a PLAQUE. Salesman of the Year." Slim pickings. It's engraved. Mostly Louis "Trent Ramsey, Car - Looks at FRAMED PHOTOS. One catches his eye. A GROUP SHOT. Among them, Trent, Kip, Joan, and Betty. AND, there's one WOMAN of interest. While everyone else is looking at the CAMERA, she's looking at Trent. But, she's turned at such an angle that we can't see much of her face, if any at all. Hanging onto her, Steph, smiling at the CAMERA. - Opens a CABINET. Practically empty, save for one old, ratty edition of CANDYLAND and a 500 PIECE PUZZLE (Something lame, like kittens in a basket.)
INT. BEACH HOUSE - DUNCAN'S BEDROOM - LATER THAT NIGHT
Duncan sits on the FLOOR. In front of him, the almost completed PUZZLE. Just two pieces left to put in. He looks around. Shakes the box. No pieces to be found. Sighs and just starts taking it apart.
EXT. BEACH HOUSE - BACK DECK - LATER THAT NIGHT
Duncan walks out, looks out over the dunes. BETTY (O.S.)
Duncan looks over at the adjacent deck, noticing Susanna, sitting in a lounge chair. She gets up, slams the screen door shut.
I don't like the way you're acting, young lady! You wouldn't be this way if your dad was here!
Feeling Duncan's stare, Susanna looks over. Caught, he looks away quickly. Beat. Checks back to see if... Yep. Susanna's still looking at him.
Beat.
Sorry about... I didn't mean to...
Oh, I don't care. It's not like we were using our inside voices.
That's cool. That's cool. Beat.
So,... You're a big fan of REO Speedwagon? ... What?
"Can't Fight This Feeling?"
Oh, no. My mom must have put that on there.
Oh, and you just got to it and thought, "What the hell. I'm going to sing the shit out of it, anyway?"
(trying to play it off) Something like that.
We hear LOUD LAUGHTER from inside Trent's house.
Sucks here, huh?
(shrugging) It's okay. It sucks. Yeah, no. Beat.
Totally.
Big time. Susanna waits for more, then...
Alright, well...
She starts back inside.
Feels like it's going to be a hot summer. Oh.
I thought we were done.
Oh,... I was thinking about that earlier... That hot thing... And, it just came to me again.
Okay. Well, if something else comes to you,... Again. I'll be inside.
Susanna heads in. Duncan collapses into a lounge chair, making himself as small as he can.
EXT. DUNES - DAWN
The sun is just coming over the horizon.
INT. BEACH HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
Duncan enters. It's a mess. Everything left out from the night before. Clearly, not everyone cleans up after themselves. Steph is there, scavenging the fridge.
They drank all the beer.
She slams the fridge door, storms out. Duncan finds a $20 BILL on the COUNTER. Next to it, a NOTE. It reads, "Up late. Sleeping in. Love you, Mom."
EXT. BEACH HOUSE (FRONT) - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
Duncan walks down the stairs.
Peter, don't get mad at me. I don't know where you're looking!
Duncan stops in his tracks. Shit. ANGLE ON Betty and Peter, at the base of their front STAIRS. Forget it!
It's gone!
Well, next time you say, "Look at the seagull," you have to remember to point!
Duncan quickly runs around the side of the STORAGE SHED, opens it. A BIKE leans against the back wall, but we can't quite make it out.
EXT. STREET - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
Duncan rides the bike. A girl's PINK CRUISER, complete with front basket and tassels. But, he doesn't care. For the first time, he seems content.
EXT. MARC ANTHONY'S - DAY - LATER
It's a PIZZA JOINT. A cool, old school vibe. Duncan leans the BIKE against the wall. Parked at the CURB, the Convertible we saw earlier.
INT. MARC ANTHONY'S - DAY - MOMENTS LATER

Duncan is at the COUNTER, buying a SOFT DRINK. Behind him, a table of rowdy GUYS, all in BLUE SHIRTS, eating pizza. We hear the familiar MUSIC of a PAC MAN GAME starting. Duncan's attention is drawn to the... BACK GAME ROOM. Another BLUE-SHIRTED GUY is playing Pac Man. EXCEPT for the classic PAC MAN upright, the rest of the video games are very 2012. Duncan approaches, standing right behind the Guy, watching.
Do you mind standing to the side? I'm getting your reflection in the screen. ... Sorry.
Duncan steps to the side. As he does, we get our first good look at the guy. It is, in fact, the DRIVER of the convertible. CLOSE ON LOGO. It reads, "Water Wizz."
I wouldn't normally care, but I'm having the game of my life. I'm on the cherry level.
But, that's the first level.
Yeah, and I still have one man left. So, like I said, it's the game of my life.
(smirks) You like Pac Man?
What? It's a classic! (re: other games) Not into all the bells and whistles, my man. Too much going on.
CLOSE ON Pac Man screen. Pac Man chases a "blinking ghost", barely devouring it before it changes back.
See, my mistake is that I get greedy. Going for all the ghosts and fruit instead of just clearing pellets.
You know, there's a pattern.
Oh, don't tell me you're one of those guys. That takes all the challenge out of it. Anybody can learn a pattern.
Just then, the blue-shirted GUYS start to get up, putting up their TRAYS.
Owen, we gotta go!
We hear the familiar sound of PAC MAN DYING.
Well, so much for the game of my life.
You still have another man left.
Naw, I've yet to see the second board. Gives me something to look forward to.
Oh, the board's always the same.
Well, there's that. Thank you. Now, I can die. Here. You play it. No, I... Go, go.
It's all you.
Duncan plays.
But, it's still my quarter. So, if you end up getting a high score, I don't want to see... What's your name?
... Duncan.
Okay, well, I don't want to see that up on the screen.
Oh, they only let you use three letters.
Wow. Nothing's left to the imagination with you, huh?
Owen starts to walk away.
Oh and hey, no pattern on my quarter, man. Cut your own path.
Duncan watches him leave.
Suddenly, we hear PAC MAN DYING
EXT. BEACH HOUSE - DAY - LATER
Duncan pulls up on his bike. Pam and Trent are loading a large COOLER into the station wagon. Steph, leaning against the car, putting on suntan lotion.
There you are. We were just about to leave without you. Where did you run off to?
(shrugging) Nowhere.
Well, let's leave notes, okay?
Your mom was worried.
Now, go put on your swim trunks. Kip and Joan invited us out on their boat. And, we're running late.
Duncan heads toward the house.
Head's up, buddy.
EXT. DOCKS - DAY - LATER

CLOSE ON DUNCAN, catching a life vest. PULL BACK to see Duncan standing on the dock. Everyone is loading stuff onto a large motor boat, all tricked out. On the back, the boat's name, "BARELY A WAKE." Trent stands on the edge of the boat.
You need to wear that.
Oh, I think he's cool without it.
No, Duncan can't swim.
(trying to make it better, clarifying) Trent, he's just not a comfortable swimmer.
I really think he'll be fine.
No, let's wear it, buddy. So, people don't have to worry about you, right?
BETTY (O.S.)
Betty walks down the dock, followed by Peter, who's carrying a BUCKET, and Susanna.
Yes, we invited ourselves. Accept it and move on. Peter has to stay
down below. His eye throws off his equilibrium and he'll get sick
No, I won't, mom!
Tell that to the bucket we have to bring!
Duncan, reluctantly, puts on the vest.
(sotto, to Pam) Why do I have to wear this? else does.
He just wants you to be safe.
Susanna walks by, lifting an eyebrow, as Pam tightens the straps on his vest.
Is that snug enough? Off Duncan's look...
EXT. BOAT - DAY - LATER

CLOSE ON Duncan, sitting. He's miserable. The life vest is three sizes too big for him. It comes halfway up his face. The adults sit around the STERN of the boat. Susanna sunbathe on the BOW.
